generic: Well, well. Another fresh face trying to make it in this Academy.
Let's see if you can handle what we throw at you.
I've been watching you wander the halls.
You've got potential. Or at least, you've got nerve. Same thing sometimes.
Honey, you look like you just stepped off a red carpet.
A very budget red carpet, but still.
You know what separates a diva from everyone else? Confidence.
And knowing every lyric to every Beyonce album. Both are essential.
I don't just battle anyone, you know.
But something about you tells me this might be worth my time.
Welcome to my domain. Rules are simple: answer correctly, or don't bother coming back.
Just kidding. We'd still let you back. Probably.
You've got that look in your eye. The 'I'm about to prove everyone wrong' look.
I respect it. Let's dance.
Some people come here to learn. Some come to win.
Which one are you? Don't answer that. Show me instead.
I heard someone new was making waves around here.
Let's see if those waves are more like a ripple or a tsunami.
Everyone starts somewhere. Let's see where you land.
Ready when you are.
STARLET: Hey! You're new here too, right?
Well, not as new as some. Let's see what you've got.
I may be a Starlet, but don't underestimate me.
I've been studying the classics since before you could spell 'discography'.
Everyone starts somewhere. For us, it's right here.
Let's prove we belong on this stage.
I love meeting new people. Especially ones who know their pop history.
Ready for a friendly face-off?
They say the hallway is where it all starts.
Let's find out if that's true.
Don't let the Starlet label fool you.
I've got tricks up my sleeve that would make a Mother nervous.
You look like someone who takes their pop culture seriously.
That's the only kind of person worth talking to around here.
I've been practicing all week for this.
Well, technically I've been practicing my eyeliner. But also this.
The Grand Lobby has seen a lot of fresh faces.
But not all of them make it to the big stage. Let's change that.
A little friendly competition never hurt anybody.
Okay, maybe it hurt a little. But that's part of the fun.
DIVA: I don't do small talk. I do big talk.
With impeccable references and zero tolerance for wrong answers.
Darling, you've entered Diva territory now.
The questions get harder, but the rewards are worth the risk.
I've defeated more challengers than I can count.
But who's counting? I am. The answer is a lot.
You've got style, I'll give you that.
But style without substance is just a costume. Prove you're more than that.
Every Diva has a signature move. Mine is knowing everything.
What's yours? Let's find out.
I don't mean to be intimidating, but I am.
It comes naturally when you have taste like mine.
Some people think being a Diva is just about attitude.
It's also about knowing every single collaboration, feature, and remix ever made.
You walked in here with confidence. I like that.
Let's see if you can walk out with the same energy.
The stage doesn't care about your feelings.
It only cares about whether you know the difference between an album track and a single.
I've been polishing my knowledge all day.
Like a diamond. Which, coincidentally, is what I am.
DJ: I hear everything. Every remix, every feature, every whispered bridge.
You can't hide a bad answer from these ears.
Music is my life. Which means my questions are about life itself.
Deep, right? Let's go deep.
I've spent more time with headphones on than without.
That kind of dedication builds a certain... encyclopedic knowledge.
You know what makes a great DJ? Knowing what comes next.
In music and in pop culture trivia battles.
I don't just listen to music. I study it.
Every sample, every interpolation, every inspired-by. It's all connected.
The connections between artists are what fascinate me.
One collaboration leads to another, which leads to another. It's a beautiful web.
My playlists have more layers than a pop star's costume change.
And every layer has a reference you need to know.
I can tell within the first question whether you're serious.
So show me you're serious. Or at least show me you're fun.
The music never stops, and neither does my trivia.
Think of it as a never-ending set. Your job is to keep up.
Some people hear a song and forget it the next day.
I hear a song and remember every detail forever. Let's see if you do too.
MOTHER: Child, this isn't regular trivia. This is Mother's test.
Think you can pass?
I've been in this game longer than most of these children have been alive.
And I've got the knowledge to prove it. Do you?
When Mother speaks, you listen.
When Mother asks, you answer. Correctly.
They call me Mother for a reason. I nurture, I guide, and I absolutely destroy in trivia.
Ready?
I don't give second chances. I give harder questions.
Consider it a form of love.
Every queen needs a mother. And every Academy needs someone who knows everything.
That someone is me.
I've seen trends come and go. I've seen artists rise and fall.
But the ones I remember? Those are the ones that mattered. Let's see if you know them.
You want to stand on The Stage? You need to earn it.
And earning it means surviving Mother's questions.
There's no shame in losing to Mother.
There is shame in not trying. So try. Hard.
I ask the hard questions.
Because I know the answers. Do you?
